architecture_fit.note: A genuine stacked self-attention circuit (adjacency-masked additive bias + identity values + per-layer support read-out, MLP dropped) — a clean small delta from base_model.py that answers the goal's attention question directly and solves the closure exactly (F1=1.0 across the sweep).

baseline_comparison.note: The adjacency 1-hop baseline is shown failing as diameter grows (1.0→0.5) while the model holds at 1.0, and depth=1 is shown to coincide exactly with that baseline — an explicit strawman-fails/method-succeeds contrast.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic with no trained model, stated plainly; the depth knob is a real causal handle (closure provably collapses to the baseline when depth<diameter, recorded in ablation.json) and the README proposes the analogous Q/K + attention-write ablation on a learned model.

operating_range.note: Depth/diameter ablation shows exactly where the circuit breaks (depth<diameter) and depth=N guarantees correctness for any graph; empirical scale is limited to the diameter {1,2,3,5} sweep (N≤24) rather than ≥2 orders of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set attention circuit with no learned parameters, expressed in torch on CUDA, reproducing exact transitive closure — strong evidence of mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab delivers both a legible F1-vs-depth line plot (per-diameter lines + baseline overlay, stepping to 1.0 at depth==diameter) and interactive colour-coded reachability heatmaps that fill in hop-by-hop against ground truth.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'Why this visualisation' section ties each panel to the core claim — depth buys reach, baseline overlaid, hop-by-hop merge — with the right quantities on the axes and matching the implementation.

notes: A strong iteration on first_pass: instead of avoiding attention, pass_2 expresses the transitive closure as a stack of adjacency-masked self-attention layers (identity values, no MLP), a clean small delta from base_model.py that nails the benchmark (transitive_closure_robustness=1.0, positive lift at every diameter>1). The depth knob doubles as a built-in causal ablation — depth=1 equals the adjacency baseline and the closure collapses whenever depth<diameter — and both the F1-vs-depth plot and the hop-by-hop reachability heatmaps make the claim legible. Main gaps are the synthetic (non-trained-model) faithfulness and the modest empirical scale range.
